title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on Who Should Buy a ULIP Plan? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: ulip plan, ulip insurance, unit linked insurance
Body Content:
Today, investment decisions may be challenging, and opting for insurance products is a confusing process. However, what if you can choose a plan that gives you life insurance cover and an investment option in one? This would be an ideal plan to allocate your funds to, right? A ULIP, or Unit Linked Insurance Plan, is just such a plan. It combines investing with insurance. A portion of the premium you pay goes into your insurance cover, and the rest is used to make investments. The premium payment’s part may not need clarification, but you may be wondering what kinds of investments are made with the other funds you allocate to the plan.
The remaining funds that you pay in a ULIP, besides the premium amount, are invested in a mix of securities, such as debt and equities. This makes a ULIP resemble a mutual fund. The consequence of this is that your wealth has a chance to grow and build into a corpus by the time your ULIP reaches maturity.
Who Makes A Good ULIP Investor?
A ULIP is a unique way to get insurance cover while you grow your wealth. What’s more, the investment portion of a ULIP may be custom-made to suit your financial needs, and you have some say in what kinds of investments may be made, and the fractions of these. Depending on your tolerance for risk and other important factors, you may wish your investments to be allocated to suit you. Does the plan sound good to you, or are you still wondering whether you would make a fine candidate to opt for such a plan? You can find out by reading below:
A ULIP Offers Financial Freedom - A Unit Linked Insurance Plan is an effective financial product for anyone who wants financial freedom. With a ULIP, there is a possibility to shift your wealth across a variety of funds. Depending on your appetite for risk, age and the returns you wish to gain, a ULIP is flexible for you as an individual. For those who want to monitor their money in real time, Unit Linked Insurance Plans are ideal. There is also transparency in transactions. Finally, ULIPs help you to attain a degree of financial freedom and the freedom to opt for investments especially matched with your requirements.
Long-Term Horizon - Any investment in equities requires patience as this is held for the long term. With volatility in the markets, good equities will only show substantial returns if you stick with them, rather than impulsively sell for short-term gains. History in the equity markets displays those good stocks can yield big returns in a time period of 5 to 10 years or more.
Age Does Not Matter - One of the ideal features of ULIPs is the fact that they offer a broad range of policies to choose from. You can get a policy to suit your age and preferences. Depending on the stage of life you are in, there will always be a ULIP to match.
Risk Appetite - The investment part of a ULIP may seem like a risky move for some investors, as that part invests your wealth in equities to a large extent. Although you can opt for different proportions of debt and equity in your investment, if you want to almost assure yourself of good profits, then equity is the way to go. For people who are risk-averse, a ULIP may not be a way to invest also. The chances of risk, either moving equities in a negative or positive direction are from 1% to 100%, although a mix of different equities may mitigate the total risk. This has to be considered when you invest in a ULIP. On the other hand, if you are okay with taking chances, a ULIP may be a perfect option for you.
Market-Based Returns - Related to risks is the part of the plan that represents equity in a ULIP insurance product. As a fraction of your plan includes the investment in the stock market, you have to account for conditions of market volatility. Volatility will affect your returns. However, there are some plans that invest in strong equities which may not be affected by market volatility to the extent that other company stocks are.
Lock-in Term - Previously, ULIPs had a lock-in tenure of three years. However, the IRDAI, or Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India increased this in 2021. The lock-in tenure now stands at 5 years. With a ULIP, to see any gains that will be beneficial to you, you need to play a patience game. If you are willing to wait to see rewards in 10-15 years, you may go in for a ULIP.
Alert Investors - With any investment product you sign up for, or for that matter, any insurance product, you must be an alert and careful investor. After reading the terms and conditions of the insurance part of the ULIP, you will have to make an investment in the other portion of your plan.
Nonetheless, even before you zero in on a plan, you must select a reputable insurer to move ahead with. Then, go in for a plan which has less fees. There are some insurers who may charge you fees for premium allocation costs, or other processing and administration expenses. Many policyholders of Unit Linked Insurance products end up paying more for plans as they have not done their homework regarding ULIPs. All policyholders and those who opt for any financial investment instruments should read documents with proper attention. The cost factor is the main thing to focus on, and you shouldn’t pay more than you need to.
Save on Tax - Investing your wealth in a ULIP gives you freedom from tax liabilities, depending on your amount of investment. In plans, your premiums or benefits from a plan are non-taxable upto Rs. 1.5 lakh, and this helps you save your money. Tax deductions are applicable if you carry forward your plan for five years or more.
Conclusion
A ULIP plan is suitable for practically anyone on the planet who wishes to save and grow their wealth with calculated risks. Such a plan also works for people who want life insurance cover and the advantages out of that kind of insurance. When you work on a financial plan for your life, a ULIP can help you to diversify your portfolio and keep you secured at the same time.
ULIPs can help you save for your retirement too. If you start your plan while you are an earning member of your family, you can afford to take more risk in terms of the investment of equity in your plan. Furthermore, if you are a ULIP policyholder and wish to take the ULIP forward into your retirement period, you can choose an option of systematic withdrawal of funds, so you are guaranteed a regular income post-retirement. With all these benefits, you stand to gain more than lose from any good ULIP when you are just starting your career. Moreover, the wealth you grow from a ULIP can be re-invested in other instruments to make your corpus even larger. With life insurance and investment plans, you should also buy comprehensive health insurance plans as part of your financial plan.
full_pdf_text:
Benefits of group term life insurance plans
A term life insurance policy helps you secure your family’s financial future in the case of any unforeseen contingency. The plan provides financial assistance to your family in your absence, helping them maintain their lifestyle and also fulfilling their financial goals.
When it comes to term insurance plans, there are three main types of policies available based on the number of individuals insured. These are as follows–
● You can buy individual term insurance plans for yourself, your spouse or dependent childrenand other relations depending upon the company policy. The plan insures one individual under the policy and hence called so. ● Joint term insurance plans wherein two or more individuals are insured under the same policy. These plans are usually available for a married couple and partners in a partnership business. ● Group term insurance plans cover a group of individuals under a single policy.
Groups favour group term insurance plans as these plans provide coverage to all the members at affordable rates. Let’s understand what these plans are all about and their benefits.
What is a group term life insurance policy?
A group term life insurance policy is one that covers the members of a recognised group. One policy is issued, which is called the Master Policy, and the policy contains the names of all the members insured under the plan.
Some of the salient features of group term plans are as follows –
● The policy is available for recognised and registered groups. For instance, employer- employee groups, banks and their accountholders, clubs, unions, trusts, etc. ● The policy is issued for a year, after which it can be renewed ● The premium of the policy can be paid by the group, by its members, or jointly ● The sum assured depends on the age, income or hierarchy of the members in the group. Alternatively, the group can opt for a uniform sum assured for all its members
Group term insurance benefits
Now that you know what the plan is all about, here’s a look at the group term insurance benefits –
1. Easy coverage availability
One of the main benefits of a group term plan is its easy availability. You get the coverage simply by virtue of being a member of the group that has invested in the policy. For instance, if your employer has bought the group term plan for its employees and you are one such employee, you would get automatic coverage under the plan. Secondly, no individual medical underwriting is done to grant coverage. As such, even older members can secure coverage under a group term plan even if they are not eligible for normal policies. Moreover, coverage would be available under the group plan even if you have a bad medical history or suffer from an illness. 2. Low premiums
Besides the easy availability, group term insurance plans have a very low premium rate. Moreover, if the group pays for the coverage of its members, you can even enjoy free coverage without having to bear the premium cost. This low or nil premium makes group plans beneficial for their members. 3. Tax benefits
If you pay the premium for the group term plan, you can claim a deduction under Section 80C on the premium paid. The death benefit received from the policy is completely tax-free too. So, besides providing coverage, group term plans also allow tax benefits.
Moreover, there are term insurance benefits for the group itself. Here’s how –
1. It can boost morale among the group members and foster loyalty and a sense of belonging. 2. Groups can show the premium expense in their tax returns and claim an exemption for the
premium paid. This reduces their taxable profits. 3. Employers can attract and retain talented employees by securing employees’ finances with a
term plan. 4. As employees’ financial concerns are taken care of, they can devote their focus to work. This
helps boost productivity.
Things to remember
Though group term life insurance plans have their share of benefits, here are a few aspects of the coverage that you should know about –
1. The coverage is available till you are a part of the group or till the group decides to keep the
coverage active. 2. You cannot choose the sum assured that you want.
So, while group term plans are beneficial, they might not give complete coverage. As such, even if you have a group term cover, it is prudent to opt for an individual term plan too. The individual plan can be customised as per your coverage needs, and you would have control over it.
So, to enjoy the maximum term insurance benefits, opt for an individual plan, too, besides any group coverage that you have. Use the term insurance calculator to find out the ideal coverage and supplement the shortfall with an independent policy for complete financial security.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789 Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility
